name: Duck Curry- Kaen of Duck
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Minced Cilantro Root (or Use Extro Cilantro Leaves)
1 teaspoon Grated Lime or Lemon Peel
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the duck, dry thoroughly and cut into serving pieces. Set aside.
2. In a deep saucepan or casserole, bring the coconut milk to a boil; simmer for 5 minutes. Pour off half and set aside, then simmer the remaining milk for about 15 minutes. Add the duck and simmer for 10 minutes.
3. Drain the chilies and pound them with salt to form a paste. Grind the peppercorns, caraway seeds, and coriander seeds together and add the chilies, cilantro root or chopped cilantro, lemon grass, lime peel, shallots, garlic, and ginger until a smooth paste forms.
4. Stir the paste into the duck and coconut milk, adding first half of the paste; taste to check strength before adding the remaining seasoning paste. Add the reserved coconut milk, a splash of fish sauce, and basil. Simmer until the duck is tender and the sauce thickens and becomes flavorful. Check seasoning and serve with rice.
5. Unused seasoning paste can be stored in a covered jar in the refrigerator for several days.
